# UC-13.4.1 · LLM API Latency and Error Rate (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Anthropic — multisource fusion, token correlation, percentile SLOs, quota class visibility)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Think of each AI model call like a courier route to a busy translation office. We measure how long deliveries take, how often they bounce back refused, and whether a whole neighborhood suddenly waits longer, so leaders see trouble before customers feel it.*

---

## Description

Detects elevated latency percentiles and error rates on managed LLM endpoints by correlating OpenTelemetry client metrics, Azure OpenAI diagnostics, API gateway invoke telemetry, and APM spans tagged for LLM vendors, enriched with endpoint and pricing metadata for tenant-aware SLO burn.

## Value

Platform and product leaders receive a defensible, multi-source narrative that separates provider incidents from tenant quota exhaustion, bad rollouts, and autonomous agent retry storms before executive dashboards and customer support queues saturate, while FinOps teams tie latency regressions to token mix shifts using the same detection rows auditors can replay.

## Implementation

Land OTel LLM client metrics, Azure OpenAI diagnostics, API gateway invoke logs, and APM spans into observability indexes; maintain llm_endpoints.csv and catalog llm_pricing; normalize tenant_id, deployment, model, region, latency_ms, and token counters; save platform_uc_13_4_1_llm_latency_error_slo every five minutes with earliest four hours; route critical and high severities to platform LLM on-call; archive weekly evidence exports and tie wrapper outputs to SLO-burn summaries.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_4_1_llm_latency_error_slo on a five-minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns across OTel, Azure diagnostics, gateway, and APM sourcetypes; alert routing to platform LLM on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab tenant, induce sustained 503 responses or synthetic high latency on a non-production deployment while forwarding OTel, gateway, and span lanes, confirm platform_uc_13_4_1_llm_latency_error_slo returns critical or high with non-null recommended_action within two collection windows.

### Negative scenario

After recovery when provider status is green, gateway error_rate is negligible, and traces show clean spans for canary traffic, confirm severities return to info or low and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with ML platform engineering, API gateway operators, cloud FinOps, identity and access management, and the observability teams that run OpenTelemetry Collectors plus Splunk HTTP Event Collector landing zones. The use case fuses four distinct evidence planes so a single silent lane cannot hide a customer-visible degradation: OpenTelemetry semantic convention metrics and histograms for outbound HTTP and generative-AI spans, Azure Monitor diagnostic settings that stream Azure OpenAI Service Resource Provider rows into Event Hub or Splunk, enterprise gateway exports such as Apigee analytics mint records, Azure API Management diagnostic logs, or AWS CloudWatch-derived Bedrock invocation summaries, and APM or distributed tracing indexes where developers stamp llm.vendor, gen_ai.operation.name, or OpenInference-style attributes on spans. Splunk becomes the tamper-resistant ledger that correlates token economics with latency percentiles, HTTP class mix, quota throttling, and deployment-level routing so incident commanders can tell a regional provider outage from a tenant exhausting tokens, a bad rollout of an API version, or an autonomous agent loop that amplifies 429 responses.

RBAC is the first hard gate. Read-only automation accounts that pull Azure Resource Manager diagnostic settings, AWS CloudTrail or CloudWatch log subscriptions, and Apigee management APIs must use workload identities or short-lived tokens stored in Splunk credential storage, not personal administrator keys on laptops. OpenTelemetry Collector service accounts need scrape and export rights only on LLM egress paths you approve. Legal and privacy reviewers must confirm whether prompts, completion snippets, or customer identifiers may appear in gateway logs; when redaction is mandatory, configure processors before HEC and document residual fields used for latency math. Network architects should verify that collectors on Kubernetes clusters can reach managed endpoints through approved egress proxies without TLS interception that breaks certificate pinning.

Index design lands complementary sourcetypes without forcing one parser to pretend it understands every vendor. Typical patterns include index=observability_metrics or index=otel for OTLP-derived metrics with sourcetype=otel:metrics or Prometheus text lines carrying http.client.request.duration and gen_ai.client.* series, index=azure_monitor or index=llm_diag for sourcetype=azure:openai:diagnostic JSON aligned to Azure Monitor table AzureOpenAIRequestStatistics or equivalent API diagnostics categories, index=api_gateway for sourcetype=apigee:mint, sourcetype=azure:apim:diagnostics, or sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch:bedrock normalized by your Terraform or CloudFormation pipelines, and index=apm or index=traces for sourcetype=otel:traces, sourcetype=apm:span, or SignalFx-compatible sourcetype=sfx_tracing when Splunk Observability Cloud content is forwarded. Retention should cover at least fourteen days hot for operational triage and thirty days for quarterly SLO attestations; legal hold may apply when pricing lookups embed contractual rate cards.

Field normalization expectations: tenant_id should represent a stable billing or security boundary such as Azure subscription, AWS account alias mapped from organization, Apigee developer app, or internal Entra ID tenant short code. deployment must carry the Azure OpenAI deployment name, Bedrock inference profile, or gateway stage that operators actually scale. model should be the provider model identifier after aliasing, not marketing names alone. region must reflect the serving geography, not only the Splunk indexer site. latency_ms must be end-to-end client-observed or gateway-measured milliseconds comparable across arms; if one lane records seconds, convert in props.conf with EVAL before this search executes. Token fields should map prompt and completion counts into gen_ai.usage.* style integers so average input and output token reporting stays comparable across OpenAI-compatible proxies, Anthropic Messages API shims, and Bedrock Converse payloads.

Risk briefing: managed LLM outages routinely split into provider-wide incidents, single-model regressions after silent router changes, customer-side quota exhaustion that looks like an outage, and application bugs that send malformed schema leading to 400-class storms. This control encodes percentile tails, short-window streamstats trend deltas, tenant-scoped eventstats baselines, explicit 429 counting, and severity ladders tied to recommended_action tokens so executives receive explainable narratives rather than raw line noise. It does not replace dedicated guardrail or content-safety analytics, secret scanning in repositories, or model evaluation harnesses; it proves whether the serving path honoring your SLO is healthy.

Differentiation recap: cross-vendor LLM HTTP latency and error analytics with token correlation and quota class visibility, not generic web front-end RUM alone, not pure cost-only reporting without latency, not security-only prompt injection classifiers without availability context.

SLO-burn companion: mirror this saved search output into a summary index keyed by tenant_id, model, and region, then drive multi-window burn alerts where error budget consumption in a one-hour window exceeds policy. Document burn thresholds in the same runbook as this detection so platform leaders can defend pacing decisions during major conferences or marketing events.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary lanes the multisearch fans wide, plus CSV governance artifacts that keep ownership and latency budgets out of SPL literals.

Lane A, OpenTelemetry HTTP and GenAI client metrics: deploy Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector agents or gateways on application clusters with an OTLP exporter aimed at your metrics index. Enable http.client and gen_ai semantic conventions, ensure http.route or server.address captures the upstream LLM host, and map llm.vendor from a static list or discovered peer name. For Prometheus scrape paths, keep histogram buckets coarse enough for percentile stability yet fine enough near your SLO knee. Sample sourcetype=otel:metrics line shape: metric_name=http.client.request.duration unit=ms value=842 http_route=/v1/chat/completions llm_vendor=openai_public deployment_id=prod-east.

Lane B, Azure OpenAI diagnostics: in Azure Portal or infrastructure-as-code, create a diagnostic setting on each Azure OpenAI account that streams RequestResponse or AllMetrics categories you are licensed to retain into Event Hub, storage, or a Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services path. Normalize OperationName, modelName, deploymentName, durationMs, statusCode, prompt tokens, and completion tokens into sourcetype=azure:openai:diagnostic. Ensure subscription and region fields join your tenant inventory without exposing full customer prompts when policy forbids it.

Lane C, gateway and cloud control-plane invoke telemetry: export Apigee mint analytics with response latency, target backend model headers, developer app identity, and HTTP status. For Azure API Management, enable Application Insights or Resource-specific diagnostics including backendDuration and totalDuration. For AWS Bedrock, land CloudWatch Logs subscription filters or curated invocation summaries with model identifier, account, Region, latency, and throttle markers into sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch:bedrock. Correlate with AWS CloudTrail for administrative changes to inference profiles that precede latency shifts.

Lane D, APM spans with explicit LLM tags: require service instrumentation libraries to set llm.vendor, llm.model, gen_ai.operation.name, and span status. Forward via OTLP to index=traces or use Splunk APM exporters. Validate trace propagation across sidecars; broken context yields orphan spans that under-count concurrency. Optional NVIDIA GPU metrics from A100 hosts can contextualize embedding batch queues when you co-locate vector work with chat inference, landed as distinct sourcetype lines tagged nvidia_gpu_a100 for capacity studies outside this core SPL.

Lookup maintenance: publish llm_endpoints.csv with columns model_id, endpoint_class, slo_latency_budget_ms, owner_pod, and optional failover_model. Refresh from service catalog jobs. Publish llm_pricing.csv or index=catalog sourcetype=llm_pricing with model_id, unit_price_tier, and notes for FinOps drilldowns. Join pricing only after detection math completes so missing price rows never drop latency evidence.

Example curl helpers for design reviews from a hardened bastion with scoped tokens:

```bash
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $MGMT_TOKEN" "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/$ACCOUNT/providers/microsoft.insights/diagnosticSettings?api-version=2021-05-01-preview" | head
curl -fsS "https://api.apigee.com/v1/organizations/$ORG/environments/$ENV/stats/apiresources" | head
aws bedrock list-foundation-models --region us-east-1 | head
```

props.conf guidance: set TRUNCATE safely for wide JSON, honor TIME_PREFIX from provider timestamps, and FIELDALIAS http.status_code to http_status_code when TAs differ. transforms.conf can hash api_key fields at ingest.

Pre-save validation: confirm each sourcetype returns rows in the last hour during business traffic, that a synthetic 429 in lab appears in gateway and OTel arms within one collection interval, and that trace exemplars include llm.vendor after a canary deployment.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_4_1_llm_latency_error_slo with a five-minute or fifteen-minute schedule depending on gateway log latency, dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical, high, or medium after deduplication. Throttle duplicate tenant_id, model, region tuples for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, status_class, and lanes in pager bodies so responders open Apigee traces, Azure health advisories, or Bedrock service dashboards without ad hoc guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch merges OTel metrics, Azure diagnostics, gateway invoke logs, and APM spans so correlated degradation surfaces even when one vendor UI lags. bucket span=5m aligns percentiles across arms before stats computes request_count, error_count, HTTP class sums, and perc50, perc95, perc99 on latency_ms. streamstats window=3 provides a short rolling trend on p95_ms per tuple. eventstats avg(p95_ms) per tenant_id builds a tenant baseline for adaptive thresholds without manual spreadsheet edits each quarter. case() encodes severity tiers responding to composite error_rate, 429_count, 5xx_count, p99_ms ceilings, and trend deltas. Enrichment joins llm_endpoints.csv for SLO budgets and llm_pricing catalog rows for cost regression triage. coalesce() fills missing tenant_id, deployment, and region fields after vendor field renames.

Pin SLO-burn alerts by scheduling a wrapper that writes summarized tuples into summary_llm_slo_burn index, then evaluates rolling thirty-minute and six-hour windows against budget curves you publish internally.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.1 managed LLM latency and error-rate fusion across OTel HTTP client histograms, Azure OpenAI diagnostic Resource Provider logs, enterprise API gateway invocation records, and APM span attributes stamped llm.vendor. Tunables earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m crit_err_rate=0.12 high_p95_mult=2.2 r429_per_5m=6")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=observability_metrics OR index=otel OR index=metrics OR index=ai_gateway) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=otel:metrics OR sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype=statsd)
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, ""))))
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(http_route, http_target, url_path, ""))))
      | where match(mn,"http.client|gen_ai|llm") OR match(route,"chat/completions|v1/chat|invoke|anthropic|bedrock-runtime")
      | eval vendor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(llm_vendor, gen_ai_system, net_peer_name, "openai_public"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(llm_response_model, gen_ai_response_model, model, deployment_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval deployment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(azure_deployment, aws_model_id, apigee_target_endpoint, ""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, subscription, organization, org_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cloud_region, azure_region, aws_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(http_client_request_duration_ms, duration_ms, value, 0))))
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(http_response_status_code, http_status_code, 200))))
      | eval in_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_input_tokens, 0))))
      | eval out_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_output_tokens, 0))))
      | eval err_f=if(http_status>=400 OR isnull(http_status),1,0)
      | eval r429=if(http_status==429,1,0)
      | eval r5xx=if(http_status>=500,1,0)
      | eval r4xx=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status<500 AND http_status!=429,1,0)
      | eval r2xx=if(http_status>=200 AND http_status<300,1,0)
      | eval lane="otel_http_client_metrics"
      | fields _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region latency_ms http_status in_tok out_tok err_f r429 r5xx r4xx r2xx lane ]
    [ search (index=azure_monitor OR index=ai_platform OR index=llm_diag) sourcetype="azure:openai:diagnostic" earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval vendor="azure_openai_service"
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_modelname, modelName, properties_modelName, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval deployment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_deploymentname, deploymentName, properties_deploymentName, ""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_subscriptionid, SubscriptionId, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_region, Region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_durationms, durationMs, 0))))
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_statuscode, statusCode, 200))))
      | eval in_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_prompttokens, PromptTokenCount, 0))))
      | eval out_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_completiontokens, CompletionTokenCount, 0))))
      | eval err_f=if(http_status>=400 OR match(lower(toString(coalesce(OperationName,operationName,""))),"fail|error"),1,0)
      | eval r429=if(http_status==429,1,0)
      | eval r5xx=if(http_status>=500,1,0)
      | eval r4xx=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status<500 AND http_status!=429,1,0)
      | eval r2xx=if(http_status>=200 AND http_status<300,1,0)
      | eval lane="azure_openai_apidiagnostics"
      | fields _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region latency_ms http_status in_tok out_tok err_f r429 r5xx r4xx r2xx lane ]
    [ search (index=api_gateway OR index=proxy OR index=waf) (sourcetype=apigee:mint OR sourcetype="azure:apim:diagnostics" OR sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch:bedrock") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval vendor=case(match(lower(toString(host)),"apigee"),"apigee_gateway", match(lower(sourcetype),"apim"),"azure_apim", match(lower(_raw),"bedrock"),"aws_bedrock_runtime", true(),"api_gateway")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(backend_model, model_id, requestedModel, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval deployment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(api_revision, stage_name, bedrock_profile, ""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(developer_app, subscription_id, aws_account_alias, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(gateway_region, location, aws_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(response_latency_ms, backend_latency_ms, integration_latency_ms, 0))))
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(response_status, status_code, httpStatus, 200))))
      | eval in_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(request_token_hint, 0))))
      | eval out_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(response_token_hint, 0))))
      | eval err_f=if(http_status>=400,1,0)
      | eval r429=if(http_status==429,1,0)
      | eval r5xx=if(http_status>=500,1,0)
      | eval r4xx=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status<500 AND http_status!=429,1,0)
      | eval r2xx=if(http_status>=200 AND http_status<300,1,0)
      | eval lane="provider_gateway_invoke_logs"
      | fields _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region latency_ms http_status in_tok out_tok err_f r429 r5xx r4xx r2xx lane ]
    [ search (index=apm OR index=rum OR index=traces) (sourcetype=otel:traces OR sourcetype=apm:span OR sourcetype=sfx_tracing) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval rawl=lower(_raw)
      | where match(rawl,"llm\.vendor|gen_ai\.operation|chat\.completion")
      | eval vendor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(llm_vendor, span_tag_vendor, "anthropic_claude_api"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(llm_model, gen_ai_response_model, span_tag_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval deployment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_namespace, ""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(organization, tenant, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cloud_avail_zone, region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms, span_duration_ms, 0))))
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(http_status_code, span_http_status, 200))))
      | eval in_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_input_tokens, 0))))
      | eval out_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_output_tokens, 0))))
      | eval err_f=if(http_status>=400 OR match(lower(toString(span_status)),"error"),1,0)
      | eval r429=if(http_status==429,1,0)
      | eval r5xx=if(http_status>=500,1,0)
      | eval r4xx=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status<500 AND http_status!=429,1,0)
      | eval r2xx=if(http_status>=200 AND http_status<300,1,0)
      | eval lane="apm_span_attributes_llm_vendor"
      | fields _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region latency_ms http_status in_tok out_tok err_f r429 r5xx r4xx r2xx lane ]
| eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, "unknown_model"))))
| eval model_id=model
| eval tenant_id=coalesce(tenant_id,"unknown_tenant")
| eval deployment=coalesce(deployment,"")
| eval region=coalesce(region,"unknown_region")
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count AS request_count sum(err_f) AS error_count sum(r429) AS r429_count sum(r5xx) AS r5xx_count sum(r4xx) AS r4xx_count sum(r2xx) AS r2xx_count avg(in_tok) AS avg_input_tokens avg(out_tok) AS avg_output_tokens perc50(latency_ms) AS p50_ms perc95(latency_ms) AS p95_ms perc99(latency_ms) AS p99_ms values(lane) AS lanes BY _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region
| eval model_family=case(match(model,"gpt-4|gpt-3.5|gpt-4o"),"openai_chat", match(model,"claude"),"anthropic_claude", match(model,"titan|nova|anthropic"),"bedrock_router", match(model,"ada|babbage|embedding|text-embedding"),"embedding_family", true(),"general_chat")
| eval error_rate=if(request_count>0, round(error_count/request_count,6), 0)
| eval status_class=case(r429_count>=6,"quota_429_storm", r5xx_count>=1 AND error_count>=2,"5xx_fault", r4xx_count>r2xx_count AND r4xx_count>=3,"client_4xx_heavy", error_count==0,"2xx_clean", true(),"mixed_http")
| sort tenant_id deployment model vendor region + _time
| streamstats window=3 global=f current=t last(p95_ms) AS p95_prev BY tenant_id deployment model vendor region
| eval p95_trend_delta=if(isnotnull(p95_prev), round(p95_ms-p95_prev,2), 0)
| eventstats avg(p95_ms) AS tenant_avg_p95 BY tenant_id
| eventstats avg(error_rate) AS fleet_err_rate
| eval severity=case(error_rate>=0.12 OR r5xx_count>=3 OR p99_ms>120000, "critical", error_rate>=0.07 OR p95_ms>coalesce(tenant_avg_p95,8000)*2.2 OR r429_count>=8, "high", error_rate>=0.04 OR r429_count>=4 OR p95_ms>coalesce(tenant_avg_p95,9000)*1.65, "medium", error_rate>=0.02 OR r429_count>=2 OR p95_trend_delta>5000, "low", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND r5xx_count>=1, "declare_provider_incident_review_regional_health_and_failover_routes", severity=="critical" AND error_rate>=0.12, "halt_agent_rollouts_validate_prompt_schema_and_key_lifecycle", severity=="high" AND r429_count>=4, "raise_quota_ticket_tune_tpm_rpm_and_purchase_capacity", match(status_class,"quota_429_storm"), "shift_traffic_reduce_concurrency_enable_queue_shaping", severity=="high", "profile_gateway_backends_compare_model_deployment_revision", severity=="medium" AND match(model_family,"embedding_family"), "separate_embedding_traffic_from_chat_and_warm_caches", severity=="medium", "diff_otel_instrumentation_versions_validate_trace_propagation", true(), "continue_sampling_trace_exemplars_verify_slos")
| join type=left max=0 model_id
    [| inputlookup llm_endpoints.csv
     | eval model_id=lower(trim(toString(model_id)))
     | fields model_id endpoint_class slo_latency_budget_ms owner_pod]
| eval endpoint_class=coalesce(endpoint_class,"unclassified")
| join type=left max=0 model_id
    [| search index=catalog sourcetype=llm_pricing earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | dedup model_id
     | eval model_id=lower(trim(toString(model_id)))
     | fields model_id unit_price_tier notes]
| eval slo_latency_budget_ms=coalesce(slo_latency_budget_ms, 30000)
| eval time=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| table time tenant_id deployment model model_family vendor region request_count error_count error_rate p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms avg_input_tokens avg_output_tokens status_class severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_4_1_llm_latency_error_slo_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-llm-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = LLM SLO $result.severity$ tenant=$result.tenant_id$ model=$result.model$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_4_1_llm_latency_error_slo | where severity IN ("critical","high","medium")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize per-arm summaries hourly into summary indexes keyed on tenant_id, model, and region, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For extremely high-cardinality model aliases, collapse rare variants through a lookup before indexing.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in the provider console: compare Azure OpenAI metrics blades, AWS Bedrock invocation graphs, OpenAI status history, and Anthropic operational notices against Splunk rows for the same five-minute bucket. When Splunk lags, verify HEC token health, Event Hub consumer lag, and collector memory limiter backpressure before muting detections.

Deep dive with exemplar trace IDs pulled from APM when gateway logs show elevated backend_latency_ms but OTel shows healthy client duration, indicating proxy buffering or TLS handshake stalls. Conversely, when OTel and spans disagree, suspect incomplete instrumentation on fallback code paths.

Quota validation: intentionally exhaust a non-production API key in lab, confirm r429_count and status_class quota_429_storm appear, then restore quota and verify severity returns to info within two windows.

Synthetic latency: run a fixed prompt against a canary deployment every five minutes from an external vantage, land results in a dedicated sourcetype, and confirm p95_ms tracks the external probe within acceptable skew.

Negative test: after recovery when provider status is green, gateway error_rate is near zero, and traces show clean spans, confirm the alert wrapper emits zero rows for critical and high filters across six consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document approved maintenance windows for model deprecations so validators do not file defects during provider-advertised brownouts.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Provider regional outage with simultaneous 5xx across tenants: declare a provider incident, shift read traffic to healthy regions, enable cached fallbacks where policy allows, and publish executive status referencing Splunk severity critical rows with r5xx_count.

Case 2 — Single-model rollout latency regression after silent router change: compare deployment revisions in gateway configs, roll back canary weights, and open a vendor ticket with p99_ms diffs extracted from this search.

Case 3 — Deployment quota exhaustion with honest 429 spikes: execute FinOps playbook to raise TPM and RPM limits, shard tenants across multiple Azure deployments or Bedrock profiles, and tune client-side max concurrency.

Case 4 — Prompt-injection or malformed tool schema producing 4xx storms: isolate offending application versions, validate JSON schema for function calls, and coordinate with security for abuse patterns without blaming the foundation model.

Case 5 — Runaway agent loops multiplying retries: detect rising request_count with static model and plummeting success in the same bucket, then apply circuit breakers and exponential backoff in orchestration code.

Case 6 — Token-cost regression when average output tokens jump: join catalog pricing rows, compare avg_output_tokens trend against release tags, and inspect decoding parameters such as max_tokens changes.

Case 7 — Content-filter false positives elevating client errors: review provider safety logs off Splunk if permitted, tune policy tiers, and separate enterprise allow-listed workloads.

Case 8 — Mismatched API version after client SDK upgrade: diff User-Agent and api-version query fields in gateway logs, pin SDKs temporarily, and document compatibility matrix.

Case 9 — Sidecar tracing not propagating W3C traceparent: fix injector configuration, verify B3 headers on internal hops, and re-run canary traffic until lanes align.

Case 10 — Fallback-model degradation when primary returns 503: validate health checks on secondary models, ensure capacity reserved, and measure latency delta with this detection.

Case 11 — Embedding versus chat traffic mixed on one deployment: split routes using model_family hints, move embedding to dedicated endpoints, and confirm GPU queue depth stabilizes.

Case 12 — Cold-start scale-up during marketing events: pre-warm provisioned throughput, raise autoscale ceilings in advance, and annotate change_ticket_id on forwarded events to explain benign latency.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.1 managed LLM latency and error-rate fusion across OTel HTTP client histograms, Azure OpenAI diagnostic Resource Provider logs, enterprise API gateway invocation records, and APM span attributes stamped llm.vendor. Tunables earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m crit_err_rate=0.12 high_p95_mult=2.2 r429_per_5m=6")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=observability_metrics OR index=otel OR index=metrics OR index=ai_gateway) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=otel:metrics OR sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype=statsd)
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, ""))))
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(http_route, http_target, url_path, ""))))
      | where match(mn,"http.client|gen_ai|llm") OR match(route,"chat/completions|v1/chat|invoke|anthropic|bedrock-runtime")
      | eval vendor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(llm_vendor, gen_ai_system, net_peer_name, "openai_public"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(llm_response_model, gen_ai_response_model, model, deployment_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval deployment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(azure_deployment, aws_model_id, apigee_target_endpoint, ""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, subscription, organization, org_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cloud_region, azure_region, aws_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(http_client_request_duration_ms, duration_ms, value, 0))))
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(http_response_status_code, http_status_code, 200))))
      | eval in_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_input_tokens, 0))))
      | eval out_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_output_tokens, 0))))
      | eval err_f=if(http_status>=400 OR isnull(http_status),1,0)
      | eval r429=if(http_status==429,1,0)
      | eval r5xx=if(http_status>=500,1,0)
      | eval r4xx=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status<500 AND http_status!=429,1,0)
      | eval r2xx=if(http_status>=200 AND http_status<300,1,0)
      | eval lane="otel_http_client_metrics"
      | fields _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region latency_ms http_status in_tok out_tok err_f r429 r5xx r4xx r2xx lane ]
    [ search (index=azure_monitor OR index=ai_platform OR index=llm_diag) sourcetype="azure:openai:diagnostic" earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval vendor="azure_openai_service"
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_modelname, modelName, properties_modelName, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval deployment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_deploymentname, deploymentName, properties_deploymentName, ""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_subscriptionid, SubscriptionId, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_region, Region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_durationms, durationMs, 0))))
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_statuscode, statusCode, 200))))
      | eval in_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_prompttokens, PromptTokenCount, 0))))
      | eval out_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(properties_completiontokens, CompletionTokenCount, 0))))
      | eval err_f=if(http_status>=400 OR match(lower(toString(coalesce(OperationName,operationName,""))),"fail|error"),1,0)
      | eval r429=if(http_status==429,1,0)
      | eval r5xx=if(http_status>=500,1,0)
      | eval r4xx=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status<500 AND http_status!=429,1,0)
      | eval r2xx=if(http_status>=200 AND http_status<300,1,0)
      | eval lane="azure_openai_apidiagnostics"
      | fields _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region latency_ms http_status in_tok out_tok err_f r429 r5xx r4xx r2xx lane ]
    [ search (index=api_gateway OR index=proxy OR index=waf) (sourcetype=apigee:mint OR sourcetype="azure:apim:diagnostics" OR sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch:bedrock") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval vendor=case(match(lower(toString(host)),"apigee"),"apigee_gateway", match(lower(sourcetype),"apim"),"azure_apim", match(lower(_raw),"bedrock"),"aws_bedrock_runtime", true(),"api_gateway")
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(backend_model, model_id, requestedModel, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval deployment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(api_revision, stage_name, bedrock_profile, ""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(developer_app, subscription_id, aws_account_alias, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(gateway_region, location, aws_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(response_latency_ms, backend_latency_ms, integration_latency_ms, 0))))
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(response_status, status_code, httpStatus, 200))))
      | eval in_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(request_token_hint, 0))))
      | eval out_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(response_token_hint, 0))))
      | eval err_f=if(http_status>=400,1,0)
      | eval r429=if(http_status==429,1,0)
      | eval r5xx=if(http_status>=500,1,0)
      | eval r4xx=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status<500 AND http_status!=429,1,0)
      | eval r2xx=if(http_status>=200 AND http_status<300,1,0)
      | eval lane="provider_gateway_invoke_logs"
      | fields _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region latency_ms http_status in_tok out_tok err_f r429 r5xx r4xx r2xx lane ]
    [ search (index=apm OR index=rum OR index=traces) (sourcetype=otel:traces OR sourcetype=apm:span OR sourcetype=sfx_tracing) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval rawl=lower(_raw)
      | where match(rawl,"llm\.vendor|gen_ai\.operation|chat\.completion")
      | eval vendor=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(llm_vendor, span_tag_vendor, "anthropic_claude_api"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(llm_model, gen_ai_response_model, span_tag_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval deployment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_namespace, ""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(organization, tenant, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cloud_avail_zone, region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms, span_duration_ms, 0))))
      | eval http_status=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(http_status_code, span_http_status, 200))))
      | eval in_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_input_tokens, 0))))
      | eval out_tok=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_output_tokens, 0))))
      | eval err_f=if(http_status>=400 OR match(lower(toString(span_status)),"error"),1,0)
      | eval r429=if(http_status==429,1,0)
      | eval r5xx=if(http_status>=500,1,0)
      | eval r4xx=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status<500 AND http_status!=429,1,0)
      | eval r2xx=if(http_status>=200 AND http_status<300,1,0)
      | eval lane="apm_span_attributes_llm_vendor"
      | fields _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region latency_ms http_status in_tok out_tok err_f r429 r5xx r4xx r2xx lane ]
| eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, "unknown_model"))))
| eval model_id=model
| eval tenant_id=coalesce(tenant_id,"unknown_tenant")
| eval deployment=coalesce(deployment,"")
| eval region=coalesce(region,"unknown_region")
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count AS request_count sum(err_f) AS error_count sum(r429) AS r429_count sum(r5xx) AS r5xx_count sum(r4xx) AS r4xx_count sum(r2xx) AS r2xx_count avg(in_tok) AS avg_input_tokens avg(out_tok) AS avg_output_tokens perc50(latency_ms) AS p50_ms perc95(latency_ms) AS p95_ms perc99(latency_ms) AS p99_ms values(lane) AS lanes BY _time tenant_id deployment model vendor region
| eval model_family=case(match(model,"gpt-4|gpt-3.5|gpt-4o"),"openai_chat", match(model,"claude"),"anthropic_claude", match(model,"titan|nova|anthropic"),"bedrock_router", match(model,"ada|babbage|embedding|text-embedding"),"embedding_family", true(),"general_chat")
| eval error_rate=if(request_count>0, round(error_count/request_count,6), 0)
| eval status_class=case(r429_count>=6,"quota_429_storm", r5xx_count>=1 AND error_count>=2,"5xx_fault", r4xx_count>r2xx_count AND r4xx_count>=3,"client_4xx_heavy", error_count==0,"2xx_clean", true(),"mixed_http")
| sort tenant_id deployment model vendor region + _time
| streamstats window=3 global=f current=t last(p95_ms) AS p95_prev BY tenant_id deployment model vendor region
| eval p95_trend_delta=if(isnotnull(p95_prev), round(p95_ms-p95_prev,2), 0)
| eventstats avg(p95_ms) AS tenant_avg_p95 BY tenant_id
| eventstats avg(error_rate) AS fleet_err_rate
| eval severity=case(error_rate>=0.12 OR r5xx_count>=3 OR p99_ms>120000, "critical", error_rate>=0.07 OR p95_ms>coalesce(tenant_avg_p95,8000)*2.2 OR r429_count>=8, "high", error_rate>=0.04 OR r429_count>=4 OR p95_ms>coalesce(tenant_avg_p95,9000)*1.65, "medium", error_rate>=0.02 OR r429_count>=2 OR p95_trend_delta>5000, "low", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND r5xx_count>=1, "declare_provider_incident_review_regional_health_and_failover_routes", severity=="critical" AND error_rate>=0.12, "halt_agent_rollouts_validate_prompt_schema_and_key_lifecycle", severity=="high" AND r429_count>=4, "raise_quota_ticket_tune_tpm_rpm_and_purchase_capacity", match(status_class,"quota_429_storm"), "shift_traffic_reduce_concurrency_enable_queue_shaping", severity=="high", "profile_gateway_backends_compare_model_deployment_revision", severity=="medium" AND match(model_family,"embedding_family"), "separate_embedding_traffic_from_chat_and_warm_caches", severity=="medium", "diff_otel_instrumentation_versions_validate_trace_propagation", true(), "continue_sampling_trace_exemplars_verify_slos")
| join type=left max=0 model_id
    [| inputlookup llm_endpoints.csv
     | eval model_id=lower(trim(toString(model_id)))
     | fields model_id endpoint_class slo_latency_budget_ms owner_pod]
| eval endpoint_class=coalesce(endpoint_class,"unclassified")
| join type=left max=0 model_id
    [| search index=catalog sourcetype=llm_pricing earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | dedup model_id
     | eval model_id=lower(trim(toString(model_id)))
     | fields model_id unit_price_tier notes]
| eval slo_latency_budget_ms=coalesce(slo_latency_budget_ms, 30000)
| eval time=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| table time tenant_id deployment model model_family vendor region request_count error_count error_rate p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms avg_input_tokens avg_output_tokens status_class severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Web.http_content_type) AS http_ct latest(Web.status) AS web_status FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web Web.url=*chat* OR Web.url=*completion* earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Web.src Web.dest
| join type=left max=0 Web.dest [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Network_Resolution.dns) AS dns_query FROM datamodel=Network_Resolution WHERE nodename=Network_Resolution earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Network_Resolution.dest | rename Network_Resolution.dest AS Web.dest]
| join type=left max=0 Web.src [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host | rename Performance.host AS Web.src]
| table Web.src Web.dest dns_query web_status http_ct cpu_pct
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by tenant_id, model, and region with drilldowns to raw OTel metrics, Azure diagnostic JSON, gateway invoke rows, trace exemplars, and lookup-enriched owner_pod; timeline of p50_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms; single-value tiles for error_rate and r429_count.

## Known False Positives

Canary traffic and synthetic probes routinely produce micro-bursts of 429 responses during quota calibration; require correlation with real tenant traffic before paging executives. Approved blue-green gateway swaps can duplicate requests for a short interval, inflating request_count without implying user-visible errors; compare trace IDs and dedupe keys. Azure diagnostic ingestion lag during Event Hub throttling can delay 5xx evidence minutes behind live provider dashboards; verify pipeline health before declaring false negatives. Apigee or API Management sampling on analytics exports may under-represent rare 5xx tails; tighten sampling for LLM routes or supplement with full-fidelity invoke logs for SLO-bound paths. Content moderation blocks that return vendor-specific 4xx codes can look like application bugs until message text is reviewed under policy. FinOps price lookup staleness in llm_pricing can mislabel cost regressions when list prices change mid-month; refresh lookups weekly. Multi-region active-active setups may show elevated latency on secondary regions by design; join region to routing policy lookups before severity escalation. Broken clock skew between collectors and providers distorts bucket alignment; monitor NTP separately. Agent frameworks that retry aggressively on 408 timeouts can spike error_rate without provider faults; inspect retry budgets. Embedding batch jobs that share deployments with interactive chat can raise p95_ms during nightly ETL; schedule segregation or downgrade non-production severity using environment tags.
